title: Podman machine start fails intermittently on MAC OS Monterey 12.3.1
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind BUG

**Description**

Podman machine start intermittently throws gives an ssh handshake error, and am unable to make use of podman post that

```
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
INFO[0000] waiting for clients...
INFO[0000] new connection from  to /var/folders/zf/wysgf9gn79l8p2755sg4zkp40000gn/T/podman/qemu_podman-machine-default.sock
Waiting for VM ...
ERRO[0010] ssh: handshake failed: EOF
WARN[0010] API socket failed ping test

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

	podman machine set --rootful

API forwarding listening on: /Users/deepak.ahire/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman-machine-default/podman.sock

The system helper service is not installed; the default Docker API socket
address can't be used by podman. If you would like to install it run the
following command:

	sudo /usr/local/Cellar/podman/4.0.2/bin/podman-mac-helper install

You can still connect Docker API clients by setting DOCKER_HOST using the
following command in your terminal session:

	export DOCKER_HOST='unix:///Users/deepak.ahire/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman-machine-default/podman.sock'

Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman machine stop`

2. shutdown macos

3. start machine and `podman machine start`

**Describe the results you received:**
 
Results received were an ssh handshake error, as a result of which podman machine shows it starts but I am unable to ssh into the system

**Describe the results you expected:**

Podman machine should start as expected

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 4.0.2
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

MAC OS Monterey 12.3.1

comments.body:
Please try with podman v4.1.
@ashley-cui PTAL
@Luap99 Sure.  Just curious, could you help understanding the cause behind this issue?

That is actually what he is trying to get at, as at the moment the issue is not clear.

Which version of qemu are you running? Would it be possible to start a VM outside of podman (eg. with qemu) to see if this all works as expected?

You could try to use the following Qemu instead: https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-qemu/releases/tag/6.2.0-1 This is what we are going to bundle with the installer on podman hopefully soon. It has been tested by several people and does not show the issue you describe

Qemu version being used is `6.2.0`. Also , after starting and stopping the machine multiple times, this error goes away.

Would it be possible to start a VM outside of podman ? - at this moment, this is not possible .
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
If you update to 12.4 this should be fixed.  The problem was between Qemu and the version of MAC.  Nothing Podman can do about it.
